title: Setting Up a Work Schedule for Employees
excerpt:

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am still learning the features of Monday.com and I feel there is a simple solution to what I am looking to accomplish.

I am attempting to set up an employee schedule when employees will be present and working. Your typical work schedule. We are a smaller non-profit and our paid employees can basically choose their own hours of when they will be working. The employees would have to be able to enter their own hours and view the hours of other employees.

I initially set up a board that has a calendar. I have it set up so the employee can enter their name, their status (Working, Work From Home, Off). I then sent up a timeline columnr and they can put the dates they are off.

I now want to change this calendar to show working hours. I am thinking of dropping the “Timeline” column and making it strictly a date so the employee would have to enter their status each day and somehow add their hours (9am-3pm), etc.

I wanted ask here to see what the most effective way is to set this up. I do not want employees to have to spend a great deal of time entering their hours if there is a better way to do it.

Any thoughts or suggestions are greatly appreciated!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have? About how many employees total?

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank You for the response. We are using Work Management with a non-profit (not sure if that matters). We are on the Pro version.

Right now, we have 4 employees that will be using this portion. We have about 50 volunteers that work regularly with us, however, volunteers will not be using this particular schedule…only the 4 employees.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I put this video together to answer. I hope it helps! Creating an Employee Availability Board for Scheduling Efficiency | Loom

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank You so much!! I’ll review it this evening. I appreciate it so much!!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Anytime! Let me know if you have questions after you look it over!

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Desiree, Thank You so much. The process is different than I expected but I do like it. I do have some follow up questions for you. I don’t know if it’s against the rules to message off the community forum, but if not, hoping to connect with you if possible.
